posts.title: NYC's Most Dangerous Corridors
posts.caption:
The BQE. The Cross Bronx. The Long Island Expressway. NYC's busiest truck corridors — and the most common places we see serious commercial-vehicle crashes.

A commercial truck doesn't carry a regular car's insurance: a private driver may carry New York's $25,000 minimum, while commercial trucks often carry $1 million or more. That changes what your case is worth — and it's why insurers move fast to settle cheap.

Hit by a commercial vehicle in NYC? Know what you're owed before you talk to anyone. Free consultation — link in bio.

Attorney Advertising. Coverage limits shown, not a guarantee of recovery. Every case is different.

posts.title: The Recorded Statement Trap
posts.caption:
Travelers and Sedgwick train their adjusters to call you fast — often within 48 hours of a crash, before you've spoken to a lawyer. There's a reason for the speed.

The call sounds helpful: "We just need a quick recorded statement to process your claim." What's actually happening is they're locking in your words while you're injured, medicated, and unsure of the facts — so those words can be used later to reduce what they owe.

Three things adjusters are trained to get you to say: that you're "feeling okay," that you're "not sure" who was at fault, and "yes" to recording the call. Each becomes a tool to devalue your claim.

Here's what most people don't know: you are not required to give the other side's insurer a recorded statement. Not for a commercial-vehicle claim, not for any claim.

If you've been hit by a commercial or city vehicle in New York, talk to someone who knows how these companies operate — before you talk to them.

You focus on healing. We focus on winning.

posts.title: 3 Things to Never Say to an Insurance Adjuster
posts.caption:
Never say these 3 things to an insurance adjuster. 👇

After a crash, the other side's insurer calls fast — friendly, helpful, "just a quick statement." What you say in that call gets used later to shrink what they owe you. Swipe for the 3 lines to avoid.

Save this so you have it if you ever need it.

Hit by a commercial vehicle in NYC? Talk to us before you talk to them — free consultation, link in bio.

posts.title: Sanitation Truck Hit You? Who's Actually Liable
posts.caption:
If a NYC sanitation truck hit you, it's not as simple as "sue the city." City-vehicle claims play by different rules — and missing one can cost you the whole case.

The big one: a Notice of Claim within 90 days. Miss that window and even a strong case can be barred. On top of that, DSNY liability works differently than a private commercial truck — different defendants, deadlines, and insurers.

Most general personal-injury attorneys don't handle these often enough to know the traps. We do — it's the core of what we do in New York.

Hit by a city or commercial vehicle? Comment below or send us a message — a consultation costs nothing.

posts.title: Why I Only Take Cases I Can Win
posts.caption:
I turn down cases every week.

If the fault isn't clear, if there's no proof, if it's word-against-word — I'll tell you straight. Taking a weak case doesn't help you; it just drags you through a fight you can't win.

What I do take: rear-ends, pedestrian hits, and commercial-vehicle crashes with dashcam or witnesses — cases where I can actually get you what you're owed.

That filter is why my clients trust me. And it's why, when I take your case, I mean it.

Hurt in a crash in NYC? Tell me what happened — free consultation, link in bio.
